children.question: 1. Curriculum
Where can I find the academic calendar?
children.answer: The latest academic calendar and the Holiday list is available on the official University of Delhi website.

children.question: What are the multiple exit options under NEP?
children.answer:
1 Year: Certificate
2 Year: Diploma
3 Year: regular Bachelor's Degree
4 Year: Honours Degree with Research

children.question: Are classes conducted in English or Hindi?
children.answer: Mainly in Hindi but also depending on the course and University guidelines.

children.question: What is the difference between Major and Minor?
children.answer:
Major is the primary domain on which your core subjects are based on. 

While Minor is an additional subject from different domain, taken alongside the Major by choosing appropriate GE.

children.question: How are internal assessments conducted?
children.answer: Internal marks are awarded by practical exams, VIVAs, assignments, attendance and activeness in class.

children.question: What is the difference between dissertation, academics and entrepreneurship?
children.answer:
1. Dissertation: Research to be done alone
2. Academics: Research to be done in a group
3. Entrepreneurship: Build a startup from scratch along with financial planning and MVP.

children.question: Where can I see my course syllabus?
children.answer: The syllabus is shared via assigned teachers or can be found online.

children.question: 2. Courses (GE/AEC/SEC/VAC)
What is GE?
children.answer:
Benefits of taking GE (General Elective) include:
1. Helps in earning minor degree in other domain.
2. A student is allowed to switch fields for postgraduate studies.

3. If your core subjects are challenging, picking scoring or interesting GE subjects can boost your overall SGPA.

children.question: How can I obtain a minor degree through GE?
children.answer:
1. Opt for 4th year of your UG programme.
2. Select GE subjects from the same domain in 7 semesters of your course.

children.question: What AEC subject will I get?
children.answer: You cannot choose. The college will alternatively assign you either EVS or your mother tongue language

children.question: Which SEC papers have only practicals?
children.answer:
- Basic IT Tools
- Programming using Python
- Statistics with R
- Essentials of Python
- Digital Marketing
- Rachnatmak Lekhan
- Rangmanch
- FrontEnd Web Development
- Negotiation & Leadership
- Creative Writing
- Yoga in Practice
- Sericulture
- E-Tourism
- Communication in Everyday/Professional Life

children.question: Which VAC papers have only practicals?
children.answer:
- Digital Empowerment
- Fit India
- Sports For Life

children.question: Can I change my GE/AEC/SEC/VAC?
children.answer: By visiting the admin office, you can get your answer!

children.question: 1. Attendance
What is the minimum attendance requirement for the college?
children.answer: As per University norms, a minimum of 67% attendance is mandatory to be eligible to sit for the end-semester examinations.

children.question: I want to get my admit card stamped. What to do when I have below 67% attendance?
children.answer:
1. If it is below 67% and above 40%, then you are required to submit an undertaking.
2. If it is below 40%, then affidavit + undertaking is required.
3. Below 10% of attendance results in a meeting with the college board members.

children.question: Is attendance compulsory for practical classes?
children.answer: All classes affect your internal marks, especially in practical heavy classes.

children.question: 2. Admit Cards & Schedules
From where I can download my admit card?
children.answer: Your admit card will be issued through the SLC examination portal as per the notified schedule.

children.question: I forgot to pay the exam fee. What to do?
children.answer: There's nothing you can do. You won't be permitted to appear in examinations and no request regarding special consideration shall be entertained by the college.

children.question: In which exam centre I have to go?
children.answer: For all subjects, the exam is conducted in your respective college!

children.question: 3. Results & Revaluation
Backlog/repeat exams
children.answer: You can apply for re-examination on the SLC portal while filling the examination form.

children.question: When are semester results declared?
children.answer: Usually after 1 or 2 months of your last exam, it is expected

children.question: How can I download my marksheet?
children.answer: You can download your marksheet on the SLC portal once declared.

children.question: How do I apply for revaluation/discrepency?
children.answer: To apply for revaluation or in case of discrepency, contact the admin office within 1 month of result declaration.

children.question: What is improvement examination?
children.answer: In order to improve grades in the last result, you are given an option to file for re-examination in order to achieve a better result.

children.question: 1. Administration & Rules
What are the admin office timings?
children.answer: 10 AM to 4 PM

children.question: How do I withdraw my admission?
children.answer:
You need to submit an application in the college office or through email on principal.arsdcollege@gmail.com. The requirements include -

1. A valid reason
2. No dues clearance from library, department lab, accounts and sports.

children.question: How to apply for inter college migration?
children.answer:
You need to submit an application in the college office once notification regarding the same is released by the University.

The requirements are -
1. You must be in second year.
2. Maintain a great CGPA.
3. Availability of seat in the desired course of the desired college.

children.question: How can I avail fee concession?
children.answer:
At the beginning of each academic session, an online form regarding fee concession and book aid is circulated through Notices. It is recommended to submit the application with appropriate documents like income certificate and other records.

Shortlisted applicants are invited for a personal interaction with the board members. The financial assistance is provided to eligible students on the basis of merit-cum-means, subject to the availability of funds.

children.question: What if I failed to submit the fees?
children.answer: Be ready for the consequences. The appropriate action includes restriction of access to college facilites and services as per college regulations.

children.question: Are visitors allowed inside campus?
children.answer: Only during college fest or similar events.

children.question: 2. IT Services
College email
children.answer:
Email: YOUR_ROLL_NO@arsd.du.ac.in (38099@arsd.du.ac.in)
Password: YOUR_ROLL_NO@FIRST_NAME (38099@ABCD)

children.question: Is Wi-Fi available in the campus?
children.answer:
Yes, you can connect to our network for network access. The names along with their passwords are listed below:
arsd-faculty - P@!admin*?
arsd-student - P@!admin
arsd-nt - P@!admin09

children.question: Can I bring my laptop to the college?
children.answer: Yes. Students may bring laptops for academic purposes, subject to college rules.

children.question: How do I reset my college email password?
children.answer: Contact the admin office for account retreival.

children.question: Wi-Fi not working—what should I do?
children.answer: Try connecting to other networks or try again in few minutes.

children.question: 3. Lost Items & Student Support
Lost personal belongings
children.answer:
1. Don't panic, share a lost & found message to all whatsapp groups possible.
2. Report the matter to the college office or security personnels.

children.question: Lost college ID
children.answer:
1. Don't panic, share a lost & found message to all whatsapp groups possible.
2. If still not found, then file an FIR at your nearest police station.
3. A duplicate card will be issued from the office upon payment of ₹100 along with the FIR.

children.question: What facilities do I get in GCR?
children.answer: Girls Common Room (GCR) is an AC room containing well furnished sofa, chairs and tables. Along with female staff support, washrooms, lockers, and Sanitary Pad Vending Machines.

children.question: Is there support for differently abled students?
children.answer:
Yes, various ramps, facilities and faculty members are available throughout the campus to help such students.

Moreover, scribes are also allowed during exams to help such students.

children.question: 1. Membership
How do I register for Library card?
children.answer: To obtain a library card, complete the admission process and visit the library desk along with your 2 passport size photos.

children.question: I have lost my library card. What should I do?
children.answer: To get a new library card, you have to submit an FIR copy along with an application and ₹20 as fine.

children.question: 2. Borrowing
How does the fine work for the issued book?
children.answer: ₹1/per day, applicable after assigned date

children.question: I lost the issued book. What should I do now?
children.answer: Purchase the latest edition of the same book and inform the library authority.

children.question: What is the maximum number of books I can get issued at once?
children.answer:
Prog. Courses: 3 books
Hons. Courses: 4 books
Masters Courses: 5 books

children.question: What is the Renewal process for the library card?
children.answer: Each year, every student is required to renew their library membership by visiting the library office

children.question: 3. Facilities
What are the library timings?
children.answer:
Mon to Fri: 9 AM to 5:30 PM
Sat: 9 AM to 4:30 PM
Closed On Sundays and Holidays

children.question: When I can access the library office?
children.answer:
Mon to Fri: 9:30 AM to 4 PM
Closed On Weekends and Holidays

children.question: What facilites does the ARSD Library provide?
children.answer:
1. Access to 1 Lakh+ resources
2. E-library facility on 1st floor

3. E-resources can be accessed through ARSD library website
4. Access to WEBOPAC

children.question: What is the library offering for differently abled students?
children.answer:
1. 10 Computers with INDO-NVDIA Software, enables screen reading
2. Two KIBO XS 2024 Software
3. Two LUNA Z00MAX 6
4. DAISY Reader
5.Angel India Device
6. Two wheelchairs with staff support
7. Laptop Issue for semester
8. CDs
9. Delhi University Braille Library Membership

children.question: 1. Scholarship
What are the required documents for NSP?
children.answer:
- Aadhar Card, Passport sized photo
- Income certificate, Bank Passbook
- Previous Marksheet, Admission certificate
- Disability / Caste / Domicile certificate (if applic.)

children.question: What are the required documents for E-Kalyan Jharkhand?
children.answer:
- Aadhar Card, Passport sized photo
- Bank Passbook
- Previous Marksheet
- Income, Caste, Domicile certificate

children.question: What are the required documents for E-District Delhi?
children.answer:
- Aadhar Card, Passport sized photo
- Bank Passbook
- Previous Marksheet
- Income, Caste, Domicile certificate

children.question: What are possible reasons for rejection?
children.answer: Incorrect info, Unreadable documents, expired income or caste certificate and bank details mismatch

children.question: Can I apply for more than one scholarships?
children.answer: No, it is not possible to enlist under more than two or more scholarships.

children.question: 2. Transport
What are the various ways to reach college?
children.answer: ARSD College is well-connected by the Delhi Metro, DTC buses, and auto-rickshaws, making commuting convenient for students.

children.question: Does the college has parking facilities?
children.answer: Yes but limited parking facilities may be available subject to college rules.

children.question: Are there any kind of concessions in the travel cost?
children.answer: DTC Bus Form must be filled for Bus Pass. While there are no concessions for metro or other modes of transport.

children.question: 3. Accommodation & Nearby Facilities
Are there any PG or hostels nearby?
children.answer: Several private PGs, hostels, and rental accommodations are available near the college.

children.question: Is stationery & printing service available nearby?
children.answer: Yes! Printing services along with stationary items, are available near and within the campus.

children.question: Are there cafes nearby?
children.answer: In the Satya Niketan market, students can visit various cafes and restaurants!

children.question: Are there any banking facilities nearby?
children.answer: There is a Canara bank within the college campus. While you can find more banks in Satya Niketan

children.question: 4. Societies
How to join a society/club?
children.answer:
Societies generally conduct recruitment drives at the beginning of the academic session and circulate them on whatsapp groups.

 Students may apply according to the eligibility criteria and selection process of each society.

children.question: Can I join multiple societies?
children.answer: Yes, as long as you can manage your attendance as well.

children.question: Will I get a society certificate?
children.answer: On the basis of active participation in meetings and involvement in events, you are guaranteed to get a certification.

children.question: 5. Sports
Where I can get sports equipment?
children.answer: You can get it from the sports room by submitting your ID card.

children.question: What sport facilities exist in college?
children.answer: You can find Football, Cricket, Basketball, Volleyball, Badminton, Table Tennis and Carrom.

children.question: 6. Events
When is the annual fest of ARSD?
children.answer: The annual fest, TIDE, usually happens around the first or second week of March.

children.question: When are the student elections?
children.answer: The student election starts around September.

children.question: How can I know about other events of the college?
children.answer: All information related to upcoming events is circulated through whatsapp groups.

children.question: 7. Placements & Career
Does the college have a placement cell?
children.answer: Yes, the college has a placement cell which holds yearly internship drives.

children.question: Are internships provided by the college?
children.answer:
ARSD provides Principle Internship (PAIS) to eligible students accross the campus. Moreover, the University of Delhi provides Vice Chancellor Internship (VCIS).

Also, the placement cell of ARSD holds yearly internship drives for the students. So make sure to join their whatsapp group!

children.question: What is PAIS?
children.answer:
Principle ARSD Internship Scheme (PAIS) provides opportunities to eligible students accross the campus.

The internship is of 2 months with ₹2000 stipend. Working hours usually go from 6 hours with one lunch break, and holidays on Weekends. The procedure is followed just like the VCIS by going through interview with the board members. 

To be eligible -
1. Your attendance must be above 67%
2. CGPA should be above 7.
